violations.text: Container(s) of medicine products were observed stored on a shelf above the back food prep area. Medicines that are in a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT for the employees are to be stored and located in a way to prevent possible contamination of FOOD, EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, LINENS, and SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. Medicines were relocated for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The door to the outside, backroom area, is not tight-fitting (gap at the base of the door with bent/worn door sweeps). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2026

The ice machine is lacking a proper air gap (currently an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer was dumped out and replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
